ac_validation.text: Given no other Forge modules exist, when the domain package is imported, then all dataclasses and enums are available with zero import errors.
Given a ProjectSpec with config={"fastapi": {"orm": "sqlalchemy"}}, when plugin_config("fastapi") is called, then it returns {"orm": "sqlalchemy"}.
Given a ProjectSpec with config={"fastapi": {"orm": "sqlalchemy"}}, when plugin_config("django") is called, then it raises KeyError.

Domain slug generation — 5 sub-cases: (1) "User Management" → "user-management"; (2) "  API V2  " → "api-v2"; (3) "a  b" → "a-b"; (4) explicit override slug="custom" respected; (5) empty name → empty slug.

Given a Question with question_type=QuestionType.CHOICE, key="orm", label="ORM", and options=["a","b"], when serialized via dataclasses.asdict(), then all fields round-trip correctly through Question(**asdict(q)).

Given a TemplateDefinition with id="fastapi-react", display_name="FastAPI + React", description="Full-stack template", backend_id="fastapi", when constructed, then all fields match and frontend_id defaults to None.
Given the domain module, when searched for imports from plugins, ui, generation, or infrastructure, then none exist.
Given a GeneratedFile with path=Path("src/main.py"), content="print('hello')", executable=True, when constructed, then all fields match.
Given a DurationEstimate with estimated_seconds=30, has_slow_steps=True, slow_step_details=["npm install"], when constructed, then all fields match.

infrastructure_readiness.notes: pytest configured via pyproject.toml (project depends on it via uv)
No test files exist yet — expected as this is the first ticket in the project. Tests are unit-only with no QApplication or plugin imports as required by pipeline.md.
Not needed — domain models are pure dataclasses with no external dependencies. Test setup is trivial (construct dataclasses inline). Only .gitkeep exists currently.
No mocks or GPU handling needed — domain models are pure Python with stdlib-only imports.

blocking_issues: CRITICAL: Question dataclass field ordering violates Python dataclass rules — `question_type: QuestionType` (no default) follows `description: str = ""` (has default), which will raise TypeError at class definition time. Fix: move `question_type` before `description` so all required fields precede all optional fields.
Same field ordering bug exists in both docs/context/tickets/001-domain-models.md (line 79 before line 78) AND docs/context/architecture.md (line 154 after line 153). Both must be corrected together.
